steps.title: Pin down the goal
steps.content: The student wants to identify the real author of Adam Bede. The answer must name the actual person who wrote the novel, not just describe the pseudonym situation.

steps.title: Find the key facts in the notes
steps.content: The notes establish that the title page said 'George Eliot,' that this was assumed to be a pseudonym, and that Mary Ann Evans later revealed herself as the real author. The correct choice must state that Mary Ann Evans wrote the novel.

steps.title: Eliminate by misread/overreach
steps.content:
• Choice A — The real author of Adam Bede was Mary Ann Evans, who published the novel using the pseudonym George Eliot.: directly names the real author and explains the pseudonym, fulfilling the goal. ✓
  • Choice B — George Eliot, which Adam Bede's title page indicated was the name of the novel's author, was widely assumed to be a pseudonym.: discusses the pseudonym status but never names the real author. ✗
  • Choice C — The title page of the novel Adam Bede indicated that the author's name was George Eliot.: reports only the pseudonym from the title page, not the real author. ✗
  • Choice D — A woman who had used a pseudonym to conceal her identity later revealed herself as the real author of Adam Bede.: confirms a woman revealed herself but never names her as Mary Ann Evans. ✗
That matches Choice A — The real author of Adam Bede was Mary Ann Evans, who published the novel using the pseudonym George Eliot..
